education.area: Transferred to University of California, Irvine

publications.name: What Makes Mental Images Vivid? Sharpness as the Key Visual Dimension
publications.publisher: The Association for Research in Vision and Ophthalmology
publications.summary: Visual mental images vary in their subjective vividness. Conventionally, this vividness has been measured on a unidimensional numerical scale. But how different visual qualities influence our experience of vividness is poorly understood. Here, we present results from a novel method for reconstructing visual properties of mental images as they vary across different subjective vividness ratings

presentations.name: Reproducible and open-source fMRI decoding pipelines for arbitrating theories of consciousness - Ali Ekhlasi, Emil Olsson, Michaela Klimova, Xueyi Huang, Angela Shen, Kiarra Garcia, Nadine Dijkstra, Jorge Morales, Megan A. K. Peters
The Components of Vividness in Perception and Mental Imagery
Improving Physical Activity Levels for Children with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD)
